The Proposal -
Promote education among tribals in the hilly and remote areas of the state of Maharashtra.
For this purpose, the government started the "Ashramshala Group" scheme from the year 1972-73.
There are a total of 556 aided ashramshalas run by NGOs out of which 37 are
Primary Aided Ashramshalas (Classes 1 to 7) and 319 Secondary Aided Ashramshalas
(Classes 1 to 10) are in progress. As well as 200 junior colleges attached to secondary ashramshalas.
(Classes 1 to 12) are in progress. 5 subsidized elementary from this academic year 2026-27
Upgradation of Ashramshalas from 8th to 10th standard and 19 aided secondary schools.
Minor Colleges affiliated to Ashramshalas (Classes 11 and 12, Arts and Science stream) approved
The matter was under the consideration of the Government.
At the meeting, The Cabinet approved the setting up of an NGO in the state under the Tribal Development Department.
Upgradation of 5 Aided Primary Ashramshalas and 19 Aided Secondary Ashramshalas
The Department's proposal to do so has been approved. In this regard, the government is now
Decisions are made as follows.
The Government's Decision-
Pursuant to the approval given by the Hon'ble Cabinet in its meeting dated 05.05.2026
Additional Commissioner, Tribal Development, Amravati and AAVP, Dharani under Tribal Development Department.
Under Shri Mahatma Mungsaji Educational Institute, Pusad, run by G. Yavatmal
Ashramshala Balapur Akhara, Ta. Academic Year at Aided Ashram, Kalamnuri G. Hingoli
Approval to start classes 8th to 10th by approving grading from 2026-27
The following terms and conditions will apply to the organization.
1. Shri Mahatma Mungsaji Educational Institute, Pusad District Yavatmal in connection with the Ashram.
Graduation classes should be started immediately after the grading orders are received. After the start of the class
Commissioner, Tribal Development, Nashik and Additional Commissioner, Tribal Development, within 30 days.
Amravati as well as the Project Officer, Integrated Tribal Development Project, Dharani.
should be reported.
2. Academic year 2026-27 to Class 8, academic year 2027-28 to Class 9, and
Classes for Class 10 should be started from the academic year 2029-30.
3. "Ashramshala" for Aided Ashramshala to the concerned upgraded institution.
Equipped with all physical facilities as per the standards mentioned in the Code-2019.
The Ashramshala and Hostel Institute has its own well-equipped concrete building.
It will be mandatory to build within two years from the date. The agency has not acted on the matter in 2 years.
If done, the grant-in-aid ashramshala will be permanently de-recognised.
4. The institution shall arrange for classrooms and accommodation in the Ashramshala in a separate building. as well as
The building should be provided with standard bathrooms, toilets, and clean water.
Ample arrangements should also be made available.
5. To raise the standard of education in the aided ashrams where grading has been approved.
It's necessary. In view of this, the annual result of the students of such ashramshalas is at least 80 percent.
At least 60 per cent of the students will be required to get first class.
6. Orders regarding recruitment to the posts to be made available by grading under the Finance Department.
They will be issued separately after approval by the High Powered Committee.
7. Sri Mahatma Mungsaji Educational Institute, Pusad, run by G. Yavatmal
Ashramshala Balapur Akhara, Ta. Kalamanuri G. Hingoli Ashram from time to time by the Government.
Subsidies will be admissible as per the prescribed formula.
8. 'Ashramshala Code' for institution-aided ashramshalas as also prescribed by the Government from time to time.
Comply with the terms, conditions, and conditions.
9. All the resident students of the Ashramshala shall be from the Scheduled Tribes.
10. If it is found that the conditions, conditions and rules prescribed by the Government are not complied with.
Also, in case of complaints of malpractice / mismanagement regarding the aforesaid ashramshala, the institution
The Ashramshala will be de-graded / de-recognised.
11. Right of Children to Compulsory and Free Education Act, 2009.
It is the responsibility of the institution to provide the facilities available to the students and to maintain the standard of education.
It will be mandatory.
12. The Institute runs all the Ashramshalas under the Tribal Development Department in the state.
Strict implementation of safety measures for students / students.
In this regard, No. 4 in the Government Decision dated 09.09.2024 herein.
The provisions will have to be strictly adhered to.
13. Reference No. 7 here. Tribal Development in Government Circular dated 07.05.2026
Aided Ashramshalas run by NGOs under the department.
Guidelines regarding admission of students should be strictly followed.
14. Commissioner, Tribal Development, Nashik to take necessary action in the aforesaid matter immediately.
The expenditure for this should be covered under the respective budgetary headings of the aided ashramshalas.
Expenditure should be deducted and met from the sanctioned grant.
